g.data-narration: Welcome to Huffman Coding - a powerful lossless compression algorithm that forms the backbone of modern file formats.
g.text: Lossless Data Compression using Greedy Algorithm

g.data-narration: Huffman coding assigns shorter binary codes to frequent characters and longer codes to rare ones, reducing storage.
g.text: • Developed by David A. Huffman in 1952

g.data-narration: Real-world applications include ZIP files, JPEG images, and MP3 audio - all using Huffman coding principles.
Prefix codes ensure no code is a prefix of another, enabling unique decoding without separators between symbols.
g.text: Definition: A coding system where NO code word is a prefix of any other code word.
This ensures unique decodability without requiring delimiters between encoded symbols.
✗ Invalid: A=0, B=01 (0 is prefix of 01)

g.data-narration: The greedy algorithm builds the Huffman tree by repeatedly combining the two nodes with smallest frequencies.
Let's work through an example with frequencies: A:45, B:13, C:12, D:16, E:9, F:5. Total = 100.
Step 1: Combine F(5)+E(9)=14. Step 2: Combine C(12)+B(13)=25. Step 3: Combine 14+D(16)=30.
Iteration 4: Combine 25+30=55. Final iteration: Combine A(45)+55=100 (root). The tree is complete!
The complete Huffman tree shows how symbols are organized: A at depth 1, B/C/D at depth 3, E/F at depth 4.
Assign codes by traversing: left edge = 0, right edge = 1. A gets 0, B/C/D get 10x, E/F get 11xx.
Average Code Length = Σ(frequency × code length) / Σ(frequencies) = 232/100 = 2.32 bits/symbol.
For string ABACABAD: original 64 bits compresses to 16 bits, achieving 75% compression ratio.
To decode: traverse tree from root, following bits until reaching a leaf. Message '0101101110' becomes 'ABACDB'.
Huffman coding runs in O(n log n) time due to n-1 iterations each performing heap operations of O(log n).
Huffman coding is provably optimal - the greedy choice of combining least frequent symbols minimizes weighted path length.
g.text: Huffman coding produces the minimum average code length among all prefix-free codes.
In any optimal tree, the two least frequent
symbols must be siblings at the deepest level
